content.text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 16:51 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to prepare a teammate-reviewable hotfix for this issue on `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push it to the local `origin`.

Keep the review branch limited to the Shape Sort hotfix, preserve unrelated local work while preparing it, and restore that local work after the push.

arguments.command: git -C /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo status --short --branch && git -C /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo branch --show-current && git -C /root/.openclaw/workspace/repo remote -v

content.text:
Done.

- Created and pushed `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` to `origin`
- Commit: `4adbfac` — `Improve Shape Sort touch targets`
- Kept the review branch focused on the Shape Sort mobile hotfix
- Restored the unrelated local work back onto `main` after the push

What changed for the hotfix:
- Increased the effective grab area for Shape Sort tiles on touch devices
- Added `touch-action: none` to the board so dragging isn’t fighting the browser
- Expanded the tile’s minimum hit area for easier phone use

Unrelated local work is still present in your working tree on `main`:
- modified `README.md`
- deleted `REVIEW_WORKFLOW.md`
- modified `notes/homepage-copy-review.md`
- untracked `.env`
- untracked `playtest/`

If you want, I can also summarize the exact diff for teammate review.
